one_liner: Start-up education, data, due diligence and deal flow in one hub.
why_now: Deal flow and diligence still run on relationships, which locks out underrepresented founders. The tooling to open that up only became cheap in the last year.

sanity.note: Operating platform with a live product and named team.

breakdown.label: Geography
breakdown.note: London is outside the Germany/DACH focus

strengths: Founded in 2021 around a focused two-sided product for founders and investors
Clear domain motivation — building the access to capital the team felt was missing
weaknesses: Small team (2–10) with no named technical or GTM lead in public data
Founder track record not independently verifiable from the deck alone
evidence: LinkedIn: Magic Sauce Online, founded 2021, 2–10 employees
Company site: magicsauce.online

strengths: Underrepresented-founder segment is real and structurally underserved
weaknesses: Education + dealflow services is a crowded, historically low-margin category
Two-sided marketplace carries a cold-start problem on both sides
No market sizing given in the deck
evidence: Deck: education, data, due diligence and dealflow hub
Several comparable platforms compete on the same wedge

strengths: Mission-aligned wedge (diversity, access) can seed a genuine community moat
weaknesses: Bundling four things — education, data, diligence, dealflow — risks doing none of them sharply
Monetisation path is unclear at pre-seed
evidence: Application: 'because we knew it could be better — more diversity, better dealflow for investors'

enrichment.one_liner: Start-up Education, Data, Due Diligence and Deal Flow in one hub

findings.summary: Magic Sauce Online provides a virtual investor readiness program and de-risking resources for founders and investors, with discounted or free access for under-represented founders and emerging markets.
MagicSauce.online is a platform designed to empower underrepresented tech founders globally by providing education, assessment, and visibility.
Magic Sauce Online was founded in 2021 and has a team size of 2-10 employees, with four associated members listed on LinkedIn.

signals: 15 projects with live URLs — ships to users

sample_posts.text:
Based on internal evals:

▪️ Kimi K3 is top-tier at cybersecurity
There is chatter on X that Moonshot benchmark-overfit. These are stealth evals. Model has raw IQ.

▪️ Sol is a leap ahead in cyber capability
At a significantly higher cost, but quite remarkable still.

▪️ Fable refuses everything
We couldn’t get it to complete the run at all. What’s interesting is that Sol in comparison was much more open to helping with defensive cyber hardening

TL;DR: frontier, open-weight cybersecurity capability is here. Try it on https://t.co/HedSpZpo1a for defensive purposes.

credibility_signals: Discussion of internal evaluations of cyber capabilities
High engagement post about the term 'AGI' and its implications

findings.summary: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch commented that the backlash to his social media post about meeting Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu hasn't cost him anything.
Guillermo Rauch is the CEO of Vercel, a $2.5 billion tech company.
Guillermo Rauch is the founder and CEO of Vercel and the creator of coding frameworks including NextJS and SocketIO.
Guillermo Rauch is the CEO of Vercel and Next.js, leading these platforms that have transformed web application development.
Guillermo Rauch is Chief Executive Officer at Vercel and Founder of Rauch Capital.
Guillermo Rauch is the CEO of Vercel.
In November 2015, Guillermo founded ZEIT, later rebranded as Vercel, focusing on cloud deployments and developer experience.
Guillermo Rauch (@rauchg) tweeted about achieving some internal success with Bun on Vercel.

enrichment_note: Live run: company article research + this founder's GitHub, X/LinkedIn and press are real. Founder is a representative stand-in (real handles), not Magic Sauce's actual team.

one_liner: Vector database that runs inside the customer's own VPC.
prior_companies: Both founders were early engineers at Celonis.
why_now: Enterprises blocked from hosted vector DBs on compliance grounds can now self-host at reasonable cost — GPU memory prices fell ~60% in 18 months, and the EU AI Act made data residency a board-level question rather than an IT preference.

sanity.note: Operating company with a public product and a named team.

strengths: Both founders shipped production infrastructure at Celonis before starting
Maintains an open-source project with 4.1k stars and steady commit history
Technical and commercial split across the two founders is clean
weaknesses: Neither founder has sold into enterprise procurement before
First company for both — no prior exit or failure to learn from
evidence: github.com/adawhitfield — 4.1k stars, 312 commits in the last 12 months
LinkedIn: 4 years Senior Engineer, Celonis (2019-2023)
YC S25 batch listing

strengths: Self-hosted inference tooling is growing well above the wider database market
Compliance pressure creates a budget holder separate from the technical buyer
weaknesses: Pinecone, Weaviate and Qdrant are all funded and moving toward the same VPC story
Bottom-up sizing rests on one assumed price point with no design partners at that price
evidence: Deck slide 7: TAM $4.2B by 2028, top-down, source not cited
Qdrant raised $28M Series A (Jan 2026) with an explicit self-hosted push

strengths: Wedge is narrow and credible: regulated German mid-market first, not 'all enterprises'
Why-now names a specific, checkable shift rather than a general AI tailwind
weaknesses: The moat described is speed of execution, which is not a moat
No answer for what happens if hosted incumbents ship the same deployment mode
evidence: Deck slide 4: 3 named design partners, all German industrials
Application answer cites GPU memory pricing and the EU AI Act

one_liner: Reconciliation engine for German Mittelstand finance teams.
why_now: The 2025 e-invoicing mandate forced every German company above the threshold onto structured invoice data. That data did not exist in machine-readable form two years ago, and reconciliation was impossible to automate without it.

breakdown.label: Desires
breakdown.note: Technical co-founder, Techstars; nothing shipped publicly

strengths: CTO was employee #4 at Solarisbank — has shipped regulated financial infrastructure
Commercial founder spent six years selling into Mittelstand finance teams
weaknesses: No public engineering footprint to inspect — one GitHub account with little activity
Neither founder has run a company before
evidence: LinkedIn: Solarisbank 2016-2021, engineering
Techstars Berlin 2025 cohort listing

strengths: A regulatory mandate creates the budget line rather than the founders having to argue for it
Mittelstand finance software is a large and famously underserved segment
weaknesses: DATEV sits between this product and almost every German finance team
Incumbent accounting suites can bundle reconciliation as a feature
evidence: Deck slide 11: 4 paid pilots at €1.8k/month
German e-invoicing mandate, phased from Jan 2025

strengths: Why-now is a hard regulatory date, not a soft trend
Narrow enough to be finishable: one workflow, one buyer
weaknesses: DATEV integration is the whole business and is not yet built
Expansion path beyond reconciliation is asserted, not shown
evidence: Deck slide 6: DATEV integration marked 'in progress'
Application answer names the e-invoicing mandate as the trigger

one_liner: Turns radiology reports into structured data for research cohorts.
why_now: Hospitals digitised reports years ago but never structured them. Long-context models made the extraction accurate enough to trust for research use in the last year.

breakdown.label: Desires
breakdown.note: Technical co-founder; no accelerator, nothing shipped publicly

one_liner: Preview environments for monorepos, one per pull request.
prior_companies: Founder maintained a widely used CI plugin for six years.
why_now: Monorepos got big enough that preview environments stopped fitting on one machine.

sanity.note: Open-source project with real users and paying customers.

breakdown.label: Desires
breakdown.note: Shipped publicly; solo founder, no accelerator

strengths: Six years maintaining a CI plugin used by thousands of repositories
The distribution channel already exists — his own users are the first customers
weaknesses: Solo founder with no co-founder named
No commercial experience; has never charged for anything before
evidence: github.com/rhyscalder — plugin with 2.8k stars, 7 years of commits
Deck slide 5: 140 paying teams converted from the free plugin

strengths: Real revenue already, which is unusual at this stage
Buyer is the same engineer who adopts it — no procurement cycle
weaknesses: Vercel and Netlify give a version of this away for free
Preview environments are a feature of CI platforms, not obviously a company
evidence: Deck slide 8: $34k ARR, growing ~9% month on month
Deck has no competitor slide

strengths: Traction is real and self-generated, so the wedge clearly works
weaknesses: Why-now is a general trend, not a specific shift — this was equally buildable in 2023
No expansion story beyond preview environments
Nothing stops the CI platforms from absorbing this
evidence: Application answer, why now: 'Monorepos got big enough' — no dated trigger
Deck slide 9: roadmap is more preview-environment features

one_liner: Contract review for German SME law firms.
prior_companies: Founder previously built and sold a document-automation tool to a Munich legal publisher.
why_now: Long-context models finally handle a full German commercial contract in one pass, which was not true 18 months ago.

breakdown.label: Desires
breakdown.note: Solo founder, no accelerator, nothing shipped publicly

strengths: Second-time founder in exactly this domain — built and sold a document-automation product
Ten years inside the buyer's world, so the ICP is named precisely rather than described
Shipped a working product before raising
weaknesses: Solo founder with no technical co-founder named
Engineering is currently one contractor
evidence: Handelsregister: prior company incorporated 2019, acquired 2023
Application answer names the buyer as 'partners at 5-30 lawyer commercial firms'

strengths: The named beachhead genuinely has budget and a compliance reason to buy
weaknesses: German SME legal software is a few hundred million at most; the deck's $8B is the global legal-tech market
Every incumbent legal suite shipped contract review as a bundled feature last year
Price point implies ~4,000 firms at full penetration — roughly the entire market
evidence: Deck slide 9: '$8B legal tech market' — global, top-down, not the served market
Bundled contract review shipped by two incumbent suites in 2025

strengths: Founder is credible enough that a pivot inside legal tooling is plausible
Why-now is real: long-context models did change what is buildable here
weaknesses: The idea as stated is a feature of a suite, not a company
No expansion path out of German SME firms is described
The same capability is being given away free by tools the customer already pays for
evidence: Application answer: no expansion market named beyond 'other DACH firms'
Deck has no competitive slide

why_now: Germany's 2025 outpatient reform pushed clinics onto per-appointment reimbursement, so a missed slot now costs them directly rather than being absorbed.

sanity.note: Coherent application with a named clinical pilot site.

breakdown.label: Desires
breakdown.note: No public footprint to check against any desire

strengths: Eight years working inside community mental-health clinics — describes the problem as an operator, not an observer
The pilot site named in the application is where she currently works
weaknesses: No GitHub, no X, no LinkedIn — nothing public to verify any of this against
No technical co-founder, and no evidence of who would build it
evidence: Application answers only — no external source found for any claim
Cold start: the usual signals do not exist for this founder

strengths: The 2025 reform changes who bears the cost of a missed appointment, which creates a buyer
Fragmented market of small clinics with no incumbent software vendor
weaknesses: Small clinics have almost no budget and buy slowly
Market size not established — no bottom-up figure given
evidence: Application answer cites the 2025 outpatient reform
Deck slide 3: 'roughly 2,800 clinics in Germany' — no source

strengths: Why-now is a specific dated policy change, not a general trend
The founder is the customer, which is the strongest form of insight at this stage
weaknesses: Nothing built yet, so there is nothing to evaluate but the description
Scheduling software is crowded generally, even if not for this niche
evidence: No product URL supplied
Application answer: pilot clinic named, no letter of intent attached

prior_companies: Two of three founders were at Scale AI.
why_now: Agent pipelines became long enough that nobody can tell which step regressed without a harness.

sanity.note: YC company with a public repository and named team.

breakdown.label: Geography
breakdown.note: San Francisco matches no thesis region — mandate is Germany, DACH, Europe

breakdown.label: Desires
breakdown.note: Technical co-founders, YC; strong on desires but out of region

prior_companies: Founder was head of merchandising at Vestiaire Collective.
why_now: Secondhand childrenswear is the fastest-growing resale category and no marketplace is built for it specifically.

sanity.note: Real company, live app, credible team. Simply not this fund's mandate.

breakdown.label: Sector
breakdown.note: 'consumer marketplace' matches no thesis tag: AI, fintech, SaaS, health

breakdown.label: Geography
breakdown.note: Paris is in Europe but outside the Germany/DACH focus

breakdown.label: Desires
breakdown.note: Adjacent to the stated 'not consumer social'

one_liner: Tokenised real-world asset custody, guaranteed yield.
prior_companies: Team lists four prior ventures, none of which resolve to a registered company.
why_now: RWA tokenisation is the biggest opportunity of the decade and we are first to market.

sanity.note: Guaranteed yield on custody is not a claim a legitimate company makes. Named team does not resolve to any registered entity, and the prior ventures cited cannot be found.
